bonuses.leadin: The first of the only two pieces by Johann Jakob Froberger that were published in his lifetime is a fantasia based on this construct. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The first of the only two pieces by Johann Jakob Froberger that were published in his lifetime is a fantasia based on this construct. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this construct that is the basis of a fantasia by Jan Pieterszoon Sweelinck (“yahn peter-sohn SWAY-link”), sometimes titled for a phrase beginning “ut” (“OOT”). It appears in the Fitzwilliam Virginal Book along with two other fantasias based on this construct by William Byrd and John Bull.
Byrd also included a hexachord fantasia in his book of virginal music titled for a lady with this surname.

This German Baroque composer’s Hexachordum Apollinis is not actually based on a hexachord. Despite his large output, this composer is now disproportionately known for a single contrapuntal piece in D major, beginning with the bassline D, A, B, F-sharp.
parts_sanitized: Name this construct that is the basis of a fantasia by Jan Pieterszoon Sweelinck ("yahn peter-sohn SWAY-link"), sometimes titled for a phrase beginning "ut" ("OOT"). It appears in the Fitzwilliam Virginal Book along with two other fantasias based on this construct by William Byrd and John Bull.
Byrd also included a hexachord fantasia in his book of virginal music titled for a lady with this surname.

This German Baroque composer's Hexachordum Apollinis is not actually based on a hexachord. Despite his large output, this composer is now disproportionately known for a single contrapuntal piece in D major, beginning with the bassline D, A, B, F-sharp.
answers: hexachord [or Ut, re, mi, fa, sol, la; accept natural hexachord; accept hexachord fantasia or fantasia on Ut, re, mi, fa, sol, la; reject “Do, re, mi, fa, sol, la”]
Johann Pachelbel [or Johann Bachelbel] (That is his Canon in D.)
answers_sanitized: hexachord [or Ut, re, mi, fa, sol, la; accept natural hexachord; accept hexachord fantasia or fantasia on Ut, re, mi, fa, sol, la; reject "Do, re, mi, fa, sol, la"]
Johann Pachelbel [or Johann Bachelbel] (That is his Canon in D.)
